# TABI

TABI is an English-language curation site introducing Japan through travel, culture, food, hidden places, and practical things worth bringing home.

## Core Topics

- [Kyoto Travel](https://tabi.guide/topics/kyoto-travel.html): Quiet shrines, ryokan, craft, and routes for seeing Kyoto with more care.
- [First-Time Japan](https://tabi.guide/topics/first-time-japan.html): Practical routes, food confidence, and gentle planning for a first trip to Japan.
- [Japanese Food](https://tabi.guide/topics/japanese-food.html): Menus, alleys, counters, convenience-store saves, and the meals that shape a trip.
- [Shopping in Japan](https://tabi.guide/topics/shopping-in-japan.html): Useful, packable, and culturally thoughtful things worth bringing home.
- [Slow Travel](https://tabi.guide/topics/slow-travel.html): Forests, islands, walking routes, and quieter places where Japan has room to breathe.

## Area Guides

- [Tokyo](https://tabi.guide/areas/tokyo.html): Neighborhood food, shopping, nightlife, and low-friction first-trip decisions.
- [Kyoto](https://tabi.guide/areas/kyoto.html): Shrines, ryokan, craft, tea, and routes that reward early starts.
- [Osaka](https://tabi.guide/areas/osaka.html): Food-first planning, markets, alleys, and neighborhoods beyond the obvious photo stop.
- [Yakushima](https://tabi.guide/areas/yakushima.html): Ancient forest, rain, coastal villages, and slower island planning.
- [Setouchi](https://tabi.guide/areas/setouchi.html): Island art, ferries, sea air, and slow travel between small ports.

## Itineraries

- [3 Days in Japan](https://tabi.guide/itineraries/three-days.html): A tight route for travelers adding Japan to a larger trip or using Tokyo as a short gateway.
- [7 Days in Japan](https://tabi.guide/itineraries/seven-days.html): A first-timer plan that keeps Tokyo and Kyoto readable without turning every day into transit.
- [10 Days in Japan](https://tabi.guide/itineraries/ten-days.html): The classic Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka route with more space for neighborhoods and food.
- [14 Days in Japan](https://tabi.guide/itineraries/fourteen-days.html): A longer plan that adds forest, islands, or walking routes after the classic first-trip core.

## Recommended Guides

- [Yakushima: Japan's Ancient Forest That Few Tourists Find](https://tabi.guide/articles/yakushima-ancient-forest-few-tourists-find.html): Yakushima is famous enough to dream about and remote enough to still feel earned. Here is how to plan the island without rushing its rain, moss, cedar trails, and quiet coastal villages.
- [A Beginner Guide to Japan National Parks](https://tabi.guide/articles/beginner-guide-to-japan-national-parks.html): Japan national parks are not all remote wilderness. Many combine villages, shrines, coastlines, hot springs, and trails that can fit into a thoughtful travel route.
- [How to Travel Japan in Summer Without Letting the Heat Win](https://tabi.guide/articles/summer-heat-travel-strategy-for-japan.html): Japanese summer can be humid, bright, and tiring, but it does not have to break the trip. Shift your timing, choose shade and water, and build the day around recovery.
- [Osaka in 3 Days: The Eating and Wandering Itinerary](https://tabi.guide/articles/osaka-three-day-eating-itinerary.html): A three-day Osaka plan built around food, neighborhoods, and enough unstructured time to follow the smell of the next grill. It balances Dotonbori energy with quieter streets that locals actually use.
- [Japanese Festivals Without the Crowd Mistakes](https://tabi.guide/articles/japanese-festivals-without-crowd-mistakes.html): Festivals can be joyful, crowded, and deeply local at the same time. Plan your exit, respect procession space, and treat the event as a community day rather than a stage show.
- [The Hidden Shrines of Kyoto Locals Still Keep Secret](https://tabi.guide/articles/hidden-shrines-kyoto-locals-keep-secret.html): Beyond Fushimi Inari and Kinkaku-ji lies another Kyoto: quiet, moss-covered, and easier to love slowly. This guide introduces lesser-known shrine routes, etiquette notes, and the best times to go without flattening the places that make them special.
- [Tokyo's Hidden Izakayas Locals Actually Go To](https://tabi.guide/articles/tokyo-hidden-izakayas-locals-go.html): The best izakaya nights are about counter seats, seasonal small plates, and not over-planning. This guide explains how to choose places that feel welcoming without needing a secret password.
- [Forest Bathing in Japan: How to Slow Down Without Turning It Into a Checklist](https://tabi.guide/articles/forest-bathing-in-japan.html): Forest bathing is less about covering a famous trail and more about letting the body catch up with the place. This guide explains how to choose a gentle forest day, read local rules, and leave with more than photographs.

## Site Data

- Sitemap: https://tabi.guide/sitemap.xml
- RSS: https://tabi.guide/feed.xml
- JSON Feed: https://tabi.guide/feed.json
